relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul enters visions reluctantly because boasting itself is not the point.
A human in Messiah is caught up to the third heaven, beyond Paul’s certainty of body-state.
The mystery remains held by the Divine.
Paradise contains unspeakable words not permitted for ordinary speech.
He will boast of such a one, but of himself only in weakness.
Even truthful boasting is restrained so no one overestimates him.
Surpassing revelation is met by a thorn that keeps pride from rising.
Three times he asks the Lord for it to depart.
Grace is sufficient; power is completed in weakness.
Weakness becomes the place where Messiah-power rests.
Their failure to recognize him forced foolish comparison.
Apostolic signs were worked among them with endurance.
The only lack was that Paul refused to burden them.
He seeks them, not their possessions.
He will spend and be spent for their lives.
He rejects the accusation of manipulative deceit.
He asks whether any envoy exploited them.
Titus walked in the same Spirit and footsteps.
All the defense is actually for their building up.
Paul fears finding disorder, jealousy, anger, slander, and inflated conflict.
He fears mourning those who have not turned from destructive practices.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 12 is the weakness revelation: visions do not crown the ego. Grace is sufficient, and Divine power rests where self-inflation breaks open.
